User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client associated with a user in the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the client was created.

Quote.title: Quote
Quote.description: Represents a quote created in the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quote entity.

status.description: The status of the quote (concept, in_behandeling, verzonden).

totaalExclBtw.description: The total price of the quote excluding BTW (VAT).

totaalInclBtw.description: The total price of the quote including BTW (VAT).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the quote was created.

Job.title: Job
Job.description: Represents a job (klus) within a quote in the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Job entity.

categorie.description: The category of the job (e.g., Wanden, Plafonds, Daken).

subcategorie.description: The subcategory of the job (e.g., HSB Wand, Metalstud Wand, EPDM Dak).

omschrijvingKlant.description: A short description of the job in plain language for the client.

lengteMm.description: The length of the job in millimeters.

hoogteMm.description: The height of the job in millimeters.

diepteMm.description: The depth of the job in millimeters.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the job was created.

JobMaterial.title: JobMaterial
JobMaterial.description: Represents a material used in a job in the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobMaterial entity.

materiaalCategorie.description: The category of the material (e.g., hout, isolatie, plaat).

naam.description: The name of the material as the user knows it.

eenheid.description: The unit of measurement for the material (e.g., m1, m2, st, pak).

hoeveelheid.description: The quantity of the material needed for the job.

opmerking.description: A comment or note about the material.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the job material was created.

Preset.title: Preset
Preset.description: Represents a preset material template for a specific category and subcategory in the HoutOfferte application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Preset entity.

categorie.description: The category of the preset (e.g., Wanden, Plafonds, Daken).

subcategorie.description: The subcategory of the preset (e.g., HSB Wand, Metalstud Wand, EPDM Dak).

naam.description: The name of the preset (e.g., Standaard HSB Wand 140mm).

materialsTemplate.description: A template containing a list of materials.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the preset was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Material entity.

categorie.description: The category of the material (e.g., hout, isolatie, plaat).

eenheid.description: The unit of measurement for the material (e.g., m1, m2, st, pak).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the material was last updated.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, adhering to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization. For instance, each document in a subcollection (e.g., `quotes` under a `user`) will contain a `userId` field, eliminating the need for security rules to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. Structural Segregation is used to ensure all documents within a collection share the same security requirements (e.g., only the user can read/write their own data). Access Modeling employs path-based ownership for private user data (e.g., `/users/{userId}/quotes/{quoteId}`), simplifying security rules. The design facilitates secure `list` operations (QAPs) by segregating data based on ownership. All data is owned by the logged-in user.
